rag.description: Delete a Google Tasks task during an automation run by resolving the target list (title or id), paging through tasks to find an exact id or title match, and then deleting the first verified match. It supports either a direct OAuth token or a stored credentials reference, enforces fail-fast checks when the list or task cannot be found, and keeps downstream steps reliable by ensuring only the intended task is removed.

qa.question: What does RemoveGmailTask do?
qa.answer: RemoveGmailTask deletes a task from a specified Google Tasks list after it finds an exact match by task id or task title.

qa.question: What parameters and key features does RemoveGmailTask support?
qa.answer: It requires TaskList and Task, supports optional Token and Credentials for authentication, resolves the list by id or title, pages through tasks, matches by id or title (case-insensitive), and deletes the first matching task.

qa.question: How does RemoveGmailTask fit into the integration context?
qa.answer: It runs as an Action in the sequential workflow model (componentType “task”) with the google icon provider and ActionRuleModel, so it can be used as a standard workflow step.

qa.question: What are best practices for using RemoveGmailTask?
qa.answer: Prefer Credentials for long-running automations, pass a list id and task id when you have them to avoid title ambiguity, and remember that if multiple tasks share the same title the first match will be deleted.

qa.question: What rule and schema details should I follow to run RemoveGmailTask?
qa.answer: Create an Action rule with pluginName set to RemoveGmailTask and pass arguments for TaskList and Task plus either {{$--Token:value}} or {{$--Credentials:value}} via the rule argument.

qa.question: What is the manifest key of this plugin?
What manifestVersion is used by this plugin?
What is the pluginType of RemoveGmailTask?
Which platforms are supported by this plugin?
Which categories is this plugin associated with?
qa.answer: The plugin is listed under the “GoogleTasks” category.

qa.question: Who is the author of this plugin manifest?
What author link is provided in the manifest metadata?
What is the manifest summary for RemoveGmailTask?
qa.answer: It deletes a task from a Google Tasks list by resolving the list by id or title, searching tasks by id or title across paged results, and failing if the list or task cannot be found to avoid deleting the wrong item.

qa.question: What sources describe RemoveGmailTask and where are they located?
qa.answer: The documentation matches the manifest behavior: resolve the list by title or id, page through tasks, and delete the first exact match by id or title. Documentation: https://github.com/g4-api/g4-plugins/blob/main/docs/Actions/RemoveGmailTask.md. Source: https://github.com/g4-api/g4-plugins/blob/main/src/G4.Plugins.Google/Actions/RemoveGmailTask.cs.

qa.question: How does RemoveGmailTask handle errors and unexpected responses?
qa.answer: It fails fast when TaskList cannot be resolved or when no matching Task is found, and workflows should handle invalid or expired tokens, credential resolution or refresh failures, insufficient OAuth scopes, API quota or rate limits, network timeouts, and non-success Google Tasks API responses.

description: ### Purpose
The RemoveGmailTask plugin deletes a task from a Google Tasks list during an automation run.
It helps workflows clean up completed, cancelled, or mistaken tasks without manual work in Google Tasks.
It supports authentication using either a direct OAuth access token or a stored credentials reference that is exchanged for a fresh token at runtime.
It reduces accidental deletions by resolving the target list first and deleting only a task that matches the provided id or title.
### Key Features and Functionality
| Feature                    | Description                                                                              |
| Delete task                | Deletes a task using the Google Tasks API (Tasks: delete).                               |
| Task match by id or title  | Locates the task by exact id match or exact title match (case-insensitive).              |
| List match by id or title  | Resolves the target task list by exact id match or exact title match (case-insensitive). |
| Task paging support        | Pages through tasks in the list until a matching task is found.                          |
| Token authentication       | Accepts a raw OAuth Bearer access token for direct API calls.                            |
| Credentials authentication | Accepts a credentials id/name and exchanges it for a fresh access token at runtime.      |
| Credentials precedence     | Uses credentials when both token and credentials are provided.                           |
| Fail-fast validation       | Fails when the task list cannot be resolved or when no matching task exists.             |
### Usages in RPA
| Use Case                    | Description                                                                               |
| Remove completed work items | Delete tasks that were completed after confirmation from another system.                  |
| Clean up duplicates         | Delete the first matching duplicate task created by a retry or a manual mistake.          |
| Cancel outdated reminders   | Remove tasks when a meeting is cancelled or a due date is no longer relevant.             |
| Enforce task list hygiene   | Delete tasks that match a clean-up rule, such as a specific title prefix or tag in notes. |

edge_cases: Google Tasks API rate limit or quota exceeded
Google Tasks API returns non-success status code
Invalid or expired OAuth access token
Missing Task
Missing TaskList
Multiple task lists match the same TaskList title
No matching task found for Task title/id in the resolved list
No matching task list found for TaskList title/id
Session.AccessToken is missing or empty
Task value is empty or whitespace-only
TaskList value is empty or whitespace-only
annotations.expected_result: The matched task is deleted from the resolved list.
annotations.notes: Deletes a task by id from a list resolved by title using a provided access token.

description: ### RemoveGmailTask: Delete by task id using access token
This example deletes a task by its id from the list `My Tasks` using a provided OAuth access token.

edge_cases: Credentials id/name not found
Credentials record missing refresh token
Credentials value is empty or whitespace-only
Google OAuth token endpoint returns non-success status code
Google Tasks API rate limit or quota exceeded
Google Tasks API returns non-success status code
Multiple tasks share the same title (first match will be deleted)
No matching task found for Task title/id in the resolved list
No matching task list found for TaskList title/id
Refresh-token exchange fails
Task value is empty or whitespace-only
TaskList value is empty or whitespace-only
annotations.expected_result: The first task whose title matches is deleted from the resolved list.
annotations.notes: Deletes a task by title from a list resolved by id using a stored credentials reference.

description: ### RemoveGmailTask: Delete by task title using credentials reference
This example deletes a task by its title using a stored credentials record, after resolving the list by id.
Identifies a stored credential record by id or name so a fresh access token can be obtained at runtime.
Avoids hardcoding short-lived tokens by exchanging stored credentials for a valid access token when needed.
Takes precedence over the token parameter so authentication stays consistent and renewable.
parameters.name: Credentials

description: Specifies the task identifier or task title that should be deleted from the resolved Google Tasks list.
Ensures the plugin targets the intended task by matching against both the task id and the task title without case sensitivity.
Prevents accidental deletion of the wrong item by requiring an exact match before the delete request is sent.
parameters.name: Task

description: Specifies the Google Tasks list identifier or list title that contains the task to delete.
Ensures the delete operation is applied in the correct list by resolving the list id from the provided value without case sensitivity.
Reduces mistakes by matching against both list id and list title before searching for the target task.
parameters.name: TaskList

description: Supplies an OAuth Bearer access token used to call the Google Tasks API.
Enables direct API access when a stored credential reference is not used.
Is ignored when a credentials reference is provided so stored credentials can take priority.
parameters.name: Token

summary: The RemoveGmailTask plugin deletes a task from a Google Tasks list during an automation run.
It resolves the target list by matching the provided list id or title, then finds the task by id or title across paged results.
It authenticates using either a direct OAuth access token or a stored credentials reference, and fails if the list or task cannot be found to avoid deleting the wrong item.
